title: Concrete Bass Telegram
prompt: Create an original post-punk track at 144 BPM in E minor, with angular downstrokes, nervous momentum, and bass-led tension. Use dry snare, tight kick, clipped hats, sparse tom hits; bass drives the song with melodic high-register lines and cold repetition. Guitars: scratchy chords, sharp single-note jabs, chorus-treated fragments, plus roomy drum ambience, tape slap, and sudden silence cuts. Vocal texture: detached, urgent, narrow, half-sung with restrained emotion. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: dry, stark, wiry, tense; mix/master: bass-forward, sharp snare, thin guitar edges, minimal polish.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop

title: Autobahn Pulse Garden

Vocal texture: cool, minimal, chant-like, mixed as another instrument.
